task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas and semicolons, treats any run of consecutive delimiters, mixed or not, as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly implements the specified logic, handles an edge case with consecutive delimiters, and has good readability.
trace: I used a list comprehension to split the input string by commas and semicolons. Then, I used another while loop to iteratively collapse any consecutive delimiters into single ones, also removing empty pieces at the beginning and end. The edge case handled here is when there are two or more consecutive delimiters with no content between them.
